labels.50: Praise or positive evaluation of something being "very" descriptive (short, special, dark, good, long, flexible, etc.).
labels.603: References to career advancement, job opportunities, or employment-related transitions in professional development contexts.
labels.633: Numbers with comma thousand separators (1,000; 14,300; 2,000; etc.).
labels.1616: Text fragments describing official institutions, departments, or organizational entities.
labels.1626: Phrases describing gradual depletion, reduction, or weakening of something over time or circumstance.
labels.2594: The feature fires on phrases where "on" appears as a preposition introducing a location, activity, or contextual setting.
labels.2600: The feature activates on tokens that are split or partially masked across the activation boundary markers.
labels.2659: Comparative or contrastive phrases introducing the second element of a "more/than" or "what/but" structure.
labels.3121: Numerical phrases describing quantities, durations, or counts (e.g., "over 25", "one in every four", "ten or eleven years").
labels.3667: Phrases describing adequacy, functionality, or satisfaction of conditions being met or maintained.
labels.3718: Adjectives or descriptive phrases describing aesthetically pleasing qualities of objects or people.
labels.4170: Numerical measurements and dates with fractional or decimal values in product specifications and timestamps.
labels.5762: Contraction "I'm" or similar pronouns followed by negation or uncertainty phrases.
labels.6148: Phrases introducing claims, assumptions, or statements that will be elaborated or explained.
labels.6176: Dollar amounts in financial contexts, ranging from millions to hundreds of dollars.
labels.6692: The feature activates on the word "Little" when it appears as the beginning of a proper noun, title, or distinctive phrase.
labels.6764: Words introducing new topics or sections, often at the beginning of sentences or clauses.
labels.7290: The feature activates on journal or publication names and citations in academic/scientific contexts.
labels.7726: Transitions between unrelated text fragments or corrupted/garbled text boundaries.
labels.7804: Product specifications and material composition details in e-commerce listings.
labels.9249: The feature activates on phrases beginning with "The" followed by a noun or noun phrase, typically at the start of article headlines or titles.
labels.9256: The feature activates on word boundaries where tokens are split mid-word or at morpheme breaks.
labels.9260: Text introductions or transitions that precede a descriptive phrase, title, or clarifying detail about content.
labels.9271: Ingredient list transitions where measurements and ingredient names are separated by punctuation or "chopped" descriptors.
labels.9845: References to products or design elements that enhance or improve a room or space.
labels.10813: Text about Lonavala as a tourist destination near Mumbai or reasons why it's worth visiting.
labels.10829: Comparisons or descriptions using "is" or "are" followed by adjectives characterizing something's qualities.
labels.11272: Dismissive or understated language introducing contrasts or qualifications to preceding statements.
labels.11853: Transitions between descriptive context and a new topic or announcement using "is/has" verbs.
labels.12809: The feature activates on phrases describing improvements, plans, or assessments that address underlying problems or health/wellness issues.
labels.12830: The feature activates on phrases describing or introducing entities (businesses, clubs, characters) with "is" or similar copular constructions followed by descriptive information.
labels.12932: The feature activates when a pipe or dash symbol appears between two text segments being connected or separated.
labels.13361: Loss or negative change events where something is lost, broken, or diminished.
labels.14367: Possessive 's followed by a noun phrase describing a characteristic, attribute, or possession.
labels.14414: Phrases describing significant change or increase that span across a line break or clause boundary.
labels.14856: Personal names or proper nouns of Indian/South Asian origin are being highlighted mid-word or at compound boundaries.
labels.14863: Describing character descriptions or attributes of LGBTQ+ characters in Glee, particularly gay or lesbian personas.
labels.14872: Conjunction or comparative phrases introducing new clauses or information (and, or, but, than).
labels.14873: Text describing something designed or built for a specific purpose or function.
labels.15493: Title or headline phrases that introduce topics, programs, or organizational announcements.
labels.16395: The feature detects the word "down" or "down-" appearing in various contexts (Down syndrome, Downpipe, down here, wind turbines health effects).
labels.16447: Temporal or historical periods and dates, particularly centuries and time ranges.
labels.16502: The feature activates on commas or conjunctions separating items in lists or enumerations.
labels.16916: Descriptions of Christmas activities, preparations, and seasonal traditions.
labels.16962: Instances where a manufacturer or technician must take action to resolve equipment malfunction or quality issues.
labels.17456: Phrases describing contentious public debates or policy disagreements, often introducing opposing viewpoints or controversial positions.
labels.17944: Hyphenated or compound words split across the activation boundary.
labels.17961: Capitalized words at the beginning of titles, headlines, or list items that introduce new topics or sections.
labels.17996: Phrases describing when or how something will be done, accessed, or completed in the future.
labels.18017: References to Los Angeles or locations/businesses in the Los Angeles area.
